body.title: Fix get_closed_tickets() — size field returns None for all tickets
body.result: get_closed_tickets() now returns size and status fields from metadata; structural auditor sees M/L/XL tickets correctly
body.intention: I intend that get_closed_tickets() returns the actual ticket size (S/M/L/XL) so the structural auditor can filter M/L/XL tickets correctly.
body.description:
lab/claudecode/completion_audit.py get_closed_tickets() returns dicts where 'size' is None and 'status' is None for all tickets. The repo_auditor filters on size M/L/XL and gets 0 matches, making T-repo-auditor-semantic-eval unable to run. Discovered when run_structural_audit() returned 0 flags despite 1800+ closed tickets in the queue.

Investigate: does get_closed_tickets() read from the correct source? Does it map the metadata fields correctly? The cc_queue stores size in the ticket JSON — check if it's being deserialized into the return dict.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/completion_audit.py (get_closed_tickets function)
**Design rules:** no SQLite
**Scope boundary:** IN — fix size/status field return. OUT — changes to repo_auditor logic or ticket schema.
**Completion criteria:** get_closed_tickets() returns dicts with correct 'size' and 'status' values; run_structural_audit() returns >0 flags against the real ticket set.
